name: Financial Acumen
description: Evaluates ability to speak the language of business outcomes, ROI, and financial impact.

criteria.name: Business Context Understanding
criteria.description: Demonstrates knowledge of client's financial situation and industry pressures

levels.label: Poor
levels.description: No awareness of client's business model or financials

levels.label: Below Average
levels.description: Surface-level understanding only

levels.label: Adequate
levels.description: Shows general industry knowledge

levels.label: Good
levels.description: References specific financial metrics relevant to client

levels.label: Excellent
levels.description: Deep understanding of P&L impact and competitive pressures

criteria.name: ROI & Value Quantification
criteria.description: Builds a credible business case with specific numbers

levels.label: Poor
levels.description: No attempt to quantify value

levels.label: Below Average
levels.description: Vague claims about savings or revenue

levels.label: Adequate
levels.description: General ROI framework but no client-specific numbers

levels.label: Good
levels.description: Specific ROI calculation tied to client's metrics

levels.label: Excellent
levels.description: Compelling multi-year business case with payback period

criteria.name: Total Cost of Ownership
criteria.description: Addresses implementation costs, hidden costs, and long-term value

levels.label: Poor
levels.description: Avoids or ignores cost discussions

levels.label: Below Average
levels.description: Only discusses license price

levels.label: Adequate
levels.description: Mentions implementation but minimizes costs

levels.label: Good
levels.description: Transparent TCO discussion with mitigation strategies

levels.label: Excellent
levels.description: Proactively builds TCO model showing long-term advantage

criteria.name: CFO / Finance Stakeholder Language
criteria.description: Speaks in terms finance leaders understand and care about

levels.label: Poor
levels.description: Only uses technical or product language

levels.label: Below Average
levels.description: Occasional financial terms but out of context

levels.label: Adequate
levels.description: Uses financial language but doesn't fully connect

levels.label: Good
levels.description: Fluent in financial language, ties to margin and growth

levels.label: Excellent
levels.description: Speaks like a business partner, not a vendor
